description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block wall composed of large, evenly sized gray stone slabs arranged in a staggered brickwork pattern. The surface of each stone is smooth but matte, with subtle variations in tone and faint joint lines filled with dark mortar. The block appears solid and robust, with no visible gaps or damage, and the joints are straight and uniform, suggesting precise construction. The color is a consistent medium gray, typical of concrete or engineered stone, and the overall structure is flat and vertical. There are no visible engravings, markings, or decorative elements on the surface. The block is likely used as a barrier or retaining wall in urban environments, providing both structural support and separation.
description: A flat, rectangular wall of gray stone blocks arranged in a staggered pattern with dark mortar joints. The surface is smooth, matte, and uniform, typical of urban barriers or retaining walls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the stone block wall with staggered gray blocks and dark joints.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the extreme thinness of the wall, appearing as a narrow vertical line.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same staggered block pattern and coloration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the wall's thin profile as a vertical line.
attribute_location_context: urban retaining wall or barrier along a sidewalk or roadway
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering or erosion; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall
